titleStmt.title: SII 38.299: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 299. (A.R. No. 299 of 1912.) ATTI, CHEYYAR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On the north wall, Agastīśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Kṛishṇadēvarāya Year: Śaka 1448, Vēya, Mithuna, śu. di. 13. Monday, Tiruvōṇam = 1526 A.D., August 20. However, the month is siṁha, not Mithuna.

summary.p: This inscription records that the tāṉattārs of Edirilichōḻachōḷēśvaram-uḍaiyār and Edirilichōḻa-viṇṇagar temples at Atti alias Kishṭṭaṇarāyapuram in Urrattī-sīrmai in Ādippaṟṟu in Perunagar-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻamaṇḍalam, the supervisory official (māḍāppattiyam) of both the above temples and the śrīkāriyam official agreed among themselves to confer the right of watchmanship (tirumēnikāval kāṇiatchi) of the idols in the temple on a certain Timmu-nāyakkar, son of Parāppat-Timmu-nāyakkar. It also records that the former sent a letter to Vaiyappa Nāyakkar and Pottu Nāyakkar, the aḍaippam officials to provide land as sarvamānyam to both the temples and these officials obtained the village Atti as sarvamānya and got it ratified by the ruler, Kṛishṇadēvamahārāya, (who was camping) on the bank of the river Kṛishṇavēṇi. The messenger, māḍāpattiyam Āṇaiyār-nāyiṉār who went to meet the emperor was given money for meeting the taxes such as sampaṇa-chāri and vāśalvechcham. The inscription also gives details regarding the shares (of land) to be allotted to the service personnel of both the temples. It is also recorded that three residential sites and one-thousand kuḻi of land were set aside for the livelihood of the watchman. It was also laid down that the recipient of the land was given the right to sell, mortagage and gift his kāṇi of land.
Tillaikūtta Bhaṭṭar, Kāmakkōḍi Bhaṭṭar, the temple accountant, Biṟumarāyaṉ of Perunagar-nāḍu, and the trustee of the Vishṇu temple, Veṇṇaikkūttar Siṅgapperumāḷ figure as signatories.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śuvamasu śvavasiRead subhamastu svastiśrī śrīmaṉ makāmaṇṭalecura Ariyarāyavipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappuvarāyirakaṇṭaṉ muvarāyirakaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātāṉ yintu rāya cuttāṇaṉ tulukka taḷavipāṭaṉ tulukkamokarantavuttāṉ yirācātirācaṉ yirāca paramecuraṉ pūṟuvatekṣeṇa paccima Uttiracatta camuttirātipati Emmaṇṭalamum koṇṭaruḷiya kikṣaṇayatevamakarāyar piṟitivi rācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷaā niṉṟa
cakāṟtdam 2 1000 4 100 4 10 8ṉ mel cellaā niṉṟa veyavaruṣattu mituṉanāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu tiṟiyoteciyum comavāramum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattunāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu veṇ(ṅ)kuṉṟakkoṭṭa perunakarnāṭṭu ĀtippaṟṟuRead attippaṟṟuUrati cirmai Attiyāṉa kiṣaṭṭaṇarāyapurattil Etirilicoḻacoḷicuramuṭaiya nayiṉār koyil tāṉakū�rumRead tāṉattārumEtirilicoḻaviṇṇakarat tem perumāḷ koyil tāṉattārum Inta Iraṇṭu koyil māṭāpattiyam ceyvār Āṉaiyārnayiṉārum Ikkoyil śrīṟiRead śrī kāriyamkāriyamceyvār pālacikṣai tapparacaiyaṉum Āka Ivvaṉaivarom
Emmil porunti kalavaippaṟṟil cattiṟapāṭi pārāpput timmu nāyakkar puttirar timmunāyakkaṟku Inta Iraṇṭu koyil tirumeṉi kāvalkāṇiāṣciRead kāṇi āṭci cutantiram paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi Atti Uṭaiyavaṟkum perumāḷukkum caṟuvamāṉiyamāka paṇṇikkoḷḷa veṇṭi Aṭaippattu vaiyyappa nāyakkaṟkum pottunāyakkaṟkum timmu nāyakkar Āḷumolaiyum kūṭṭi māṭāpattiyam Āṇaiyār nayiṉāṟku campaṇacāriyum vācalveccattukku poṉṉum kuṭuttu Aṉuppukaiyil Itu muṉṉilaiyāka cuvāmi
kiṣṭṭaṇa teva makarāyar pātam kaṇṭu kiṣṭṭaṇaveṇikkaraiyile Uṭaiyavaṟkum perumāḷukkum Atti caṟuvamāṉiyam paṇṇik koṇṭuvarukaiyil perunakarile cemattiṭaiyāyirunta teva kāraṅkaḷaiyum tammuṭaiya taṉmokaRead taṉmamākaEḻuntaruḷap paṇṇa vinava piratiṣṭṭai paṇṇi vaikkayil Inta Iraṇṭu koyil tirumeṉi kāval kāṇiāṣciRead kāṇi āṭcicutantiramum cālaivācal kāttaṟaivācalum vicumuttiraiyum cemum teyvarāciyamum maṟṟum Uṇṭāṉa paṇṭi paṇṭāramum cantiṟātittavaraiyum kāttukkoṇṭu koyilcutantiram Uṇṭāṉattukku Uṭaiyavar koyilukku
tāṉattār nālu paṅkum māṭapattiyam Āṇaiyaṉnayiṉār Oru paṅkum tām Oru paṅkum Āka Āṟupaṅku Ākavum perumāḷ koyilukku tāṉattār Iraṇṭu paṅkum māṭapattiyam Āṇaiyarnayinār Oru paṅkum tām Oru paṅkum Āka nālu paṅku Ākavum vanta lāpalopam Uṇṭāṉatum Inta paṅku viḻukkāṭṭile Aṉupavittuk koḷḷak kaṭaivom Ākavum Itukku tirumeṉi kāvalkāṇi ĀṣcikkuRead āṭcikkutamakku paṇṇi kuṭutta kaṭṭaḷai perumāḷ koyil caṉṉatikellai
vaṭaciṟakil nāracattukku meṟku tevatāṉattil maṉaiyiraṇṭum tiruviṭaiyāṭṭattil maṉai Oṉṟum Āka maṉai muṉṟukku Aṭi toṇṇūṟum Uṭaiyavar koyil tirumeṉikāvalukku viṭṭa māṉipatilRead māṉiyattiltevatāṉattil Erikiḻ nañcaippaṟṟu peruceṟu 9 10 2 kuḻi 2 100 3 10 3 mukkāl melaikarumpaṉ kuḻi tma5 pañcappataṟil Āṟṟaṅkaraiyil kuḻi 3 100 3 10 perumāḷ koyil tirumeṉikāvalukku viṭṭa māṉipalaRead māṉiyattil tiruviṭaiaṭṭatilRead tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam Eriki
ḻ nañcaippaṟṟil vaṭṭam kuḻi 100 7 10 5 Ipuñcaippaṟṟu vāḷvāṅkiṉāṉ tākkil kuḻi 100 7 10 Āka patiṉeṭṭu Aṭikkolāl nañcai puñcaiyil kuḻi Āyiramum cantiṟātittavaraiyum Aṉupavittukkoḷḷak kaṭaiyvarākavamRead kaṭaiyvārākavumInta koyilvāyilukaḷukku Irācika teyvikamāka Enta kāriyam vantatu Uṇṭāṉālum nāṅkaḷ tāmumāka Eṟiṉa maṇṭalamum Eṟi Uttiram colli niṟuvacikkaRead niṟuvakikkakoḷḷak kaṭaivomākavum yinta tirumeṉikāvalkāṇiākṣi Ivaṟku
viṟṟu Oṟṟip piṟitik kiraiyatāya tāṉataṉmaṅkaḷukkum maṟṟum Eppeṟpaṭṭa cakalappiṟattikaḷukkum varittakaṭaivatākavum Itukku Eṅkaḷukkuḷḷe yātoruttar Akitam niṉaitta peruṇṭāṉāl Inta koyilpaṇṭārattukku turokiyumāy kāṇiāṣci cutāra taramumRead kāṇi āṭci cutantiramumviṭṭupoka kaṭaivatākavum Ippiramāṇam kalveṭṭiṉattukku Eḻuttuppiḻai vācakappaḻutu varimāṟāṭṭam maṟṟum Epperpaṭṭa toṣaṅkaḷum colla piṟatākavum
piṟattiyile yātoruvar Akitam coṉṉa per Uṇṭāṉāl keṅkaik karaiyil kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa toṣattile poka kaṭaivavarākavumRead kaṭaivarākavumIppaṭikku cantiṟātittavaraiyum Aṉupavittu cukattileyirukkavum § Ivai tiruvekampapaṭṭar Eḻuttu § Ivai tillaikkūttapaṭṭar Eḻuttu §
Ivai kāmakkoṭipaṭṭar Eḻuttu Ivai tirucciṟṟampalapaṭṭar Eḻuttu Ivai perumāḷkoyil tāṉattār veṇṇaikkūttar ciṅkapperumāḷ Eḻuttu §
Ivai māṭāpattiyam Āṇaiyar nayiṉār Eḻuttu Ivarkaḷ colla Eḻutiṉamaikku koyilkaṇakku perunakarnāṭṭu piṟumarāyaṉ Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 38.299 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
